rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. BIRD IN HAND,INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn An Joint Mtn To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And To Enter Stipulation And (Proposed) Order Re: Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT BIRD-IN-HAND, INC.'S Joint MOTION To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement Re: Consent Judgment: HEARING REQUIRED. THE SUPPLEMENTAL MARTIN DECLARATION FAILS TO ATTACH EXHIBIT A. THE COURT DOES NOT UNDERSTAND PARAGRAPH ELEVEN OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL MARTIN DECLARATION. DID DEA SERVICES PARTICIPATE IN THE INVESTIGATION OF THE MANUFACTURER DESCRIBED IN MARTIN PARAGRAPH ELEVEN? IF SO, HOW MANY RETAILERS OF THAT MANUFACTURER'S PRODUCTS HAVE BEEN SUED FOR PROPOSITION 65 VIOLATIONS? =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. LION SUPERMARKET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Joint Mtn And Joint Mtn To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And To Enter Stipulation And (Proposed) Order Re: Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT LION SUPERMARKET'S Joint MOTION To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement Re: Consent Judgment: HEARING REQUIRED. EXHIBIT A TO MARTIN SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION HAS NOT BEEN FILED. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE MICHIELS VS. STEVEN RICHARDS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS STEVEN RICHARDS AND ACCORD INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THAT RICHARDS INTENTIONALLY MISREPRESENTED THE OWNER OF THE SCHOOL TO OBTAIN A BENEFIT FROM THE SALE OF THE POLICY TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR IDENTITY THEFT UNDER C.C. SECTION 3344 AND/OR B&P CODE SECTION 17200. PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A CLAIM UNDER C.C. SECTION 1798.93. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE MICHIELS VS. STEVEN RICHARDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltf'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS STEVEN RICHARDS AND ACCORD INSURANCE SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of PLAINTIFF'S First Amended Complaint IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ATTORNEYS' FEES. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS AS TO ALLEGATIONS RELATED TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD INTENTIONAL CONDUCT ON THE PART OF RICHARDS. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP MILLENBAH VS. GREG BURKE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANT GREG BURKE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED; TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HOW PLAINTIFF WAS DAMAGED AS A RESULT OF THE ALLEGED BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY THAT OCCURRED AFTER THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WAS TERMINATED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC., IN THE INTEREST OF VS. GORDON MOTT ROOFING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judicial Approval Of Settlement Between Pltf Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. And Deft Sf A-1 Roofing & Maintenance Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 8, 2007, LINE 9. PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP, INC.'S MOTION For Judicial Approval Of Settlement Between PLAINTIFF Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. And DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO A-1 Roofing & Maintenance Co., Inc.: HEARING REQUIRED. PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE EVIDENCE THAT THE ATTORNEYS' FEES REQUESTED ARE CONSISTENT WITH MARKET RATES. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE DETAILED INFORMATION ABOUT C.A.G.'S NON-PROFIT ACTIVITIES AND A STATEMENT UNDER OATH THAT THERE IS NO PERSONAL OR BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN COUNSEL AND C.A.G. =(302/PJM/PB)
